steps.title: Check where the new point falls on the original trend
steps.content:
Using the original line $y = -5.1 + 8.7x$, predict the weight at $x = 9$:
$$y = -5.1 + 8.7(9) = -5.1 + 78.3 = 73.2$$
The new data point $(9, 52)$ is 21 pounds below this prediction. This significant outlier lies well below the original trend line and will pull the regression downward when added to the data set.

steps.title: Determine how the slope changes
steps.content:
When an outlier below the trend is added to a regression, the slope decreases. Only choice A ($5.9$) is less than the original slope of $8.7$, making it the only viable candidate among the choices.

To verify this estimate makes sense, note that the regression must now fit the data roughly from the leftmost point $(2, 12)$ to the new rightmost point $(9, 52)$. A quick slope estimate:
$$\text{slope} \approx \frac{52 - 12}{9 - 2} = \frac{40}{7} \approx 5.71$$
This is very close to $5.9$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
